mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand expansion, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months with rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction during high inflation regimes (CPIAUCSL > 5%).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 3.0 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of months when CPI exceeds 3.0%, or the signal fails to predict returns after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer income and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in a logistic regression of return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with above-trend GDP, or the signal shows no predictive power after adjusting for prior market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when oil prices are stable.
